hero_subtitle: SFTY connects compliance providers and regulatory rules to structure ADGM-specific workflows, preserving audit evidence and streamlining case management for digital asset businesses.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Compliance slows when providers remain disconnected
problem_description: Compliance teams managing ADGM obligations face delays and inefficiencies as onboarding, Travel Rule, screening and case workflows rely on fragmented provider outputs. This separation weakens audit readiness and makes it harder to trace decisions across jurisdiction-specific compliance processes.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects onboarding, Travel Rule, screening and case data into one operational layer that aligns with ADGM policies and preserves audit evidence across jurisdiction-specific compliance steps.
workflow_title: From provider checks to ADGM compliance decisions
workflow_description: SFTY connects onboarding, KYC, KYB and Travel Rule provider outputs through rules and case management into compliant decisions and audit-ready evidence for ADGM workflows.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects ADGM provider outputs into structured workflows, cases, decisions, evidence, and reporting without disrupting existing compliance tools or processes.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs managing ADGM obligations with controlled provider integration, audit-ready evidence and enterprise deployment for scalable, defensible compliance operations.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance

ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit-ready evidence supports ADGM compliance reviews

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for ADGM compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY supports compliance teams managing ADGM rules by connecting provider outputs into connected workflows, regulatory monitoring, case management and audit-ready evidence for jurisdiction-specific obligations.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY orchestrate ADGM providers?
faq_2_answer: SFTY aggregates data from ADGM-regulated KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers to enforce internal policies and create structured reviews, cases and reports.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves a full audit trail with decision logs, case histories and evidence packages that support operational traceability and review readiness for ADGM compliance.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance stacks?
faq_4_answer: Yes, SFTY connects seamlessly to existing provider tools and internal systems, layering orchestration, workflow control, audit logging and regulatory rules monitoring without replacing current stacks.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace ADGM compliance providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY sits above ADGM compliance providers to orchestrate their outputs into structured workflows and reports, rather than replacing underlying provider services.
final_title: Connect ADGM compliance providers into one workflow
final_description: Review how SFTY structures provider outputs, regulatory rules and cases into audit-ready workflows for ADGM-focused VASPs.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.

footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
